mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon. This reflects a healthy consumption environment driving economic expansion.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, especially from a low base, signals an impending economic slowdown or recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline due to reduced consumer spending and business activity.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high (e.g., above 5%) and retail sales growth is simultaneously decelerating, it indicates that tight monetary policy is effectively slowing consumer demand, which will negatively impact corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over the longer term.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% and RSAFS 3-month growth is below 0.5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong housing market activity, indicated by consistently high housing starts, investor confidence in future economic growth and consumer wealth effects drives positive S&P 500 returns, reflecting broad economic optimism.
failure_condition: If HOUST remains above 1500k units for 3 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of those periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector, indicating broader economic weakness that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns as demand for goods diminishes.
failure_condition: If INDPRO falls by more than 2.0% annualized over 3 months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons due to increased economic activity.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising rapidly while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is very low and the federal funds rate is already elevated, it signals an overheating economy likely to trigger aggressive monetary tightening, which will negatively impact equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4.0% AND UNRATE < 5.0% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
regime_hint: overheating economy with tight labor market
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4.0% annualized, UNRATE is below 5.0%, and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust GDP growth, the overall economic expansion provides a strong tailwind for corporate earnings across sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence in future profitability increases.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.
